reference_answer: OSHRC Docket No. 23-0121.
Precision Refractory Services, LLC; refractory and fireproofing work (laying fireproof or insulated brick inside heaters, boilers, furnaces, and similar structures).
Administrative Law Judge Heather A. Joys.
Nicole A. Spain, Esq., Madison J. Monzon, Esq., and Rachel Graeber, Esq. (U.S. Department of Labor, Office of the Solicitor, Atlanta, GA).
Steven O. Grubbs, Esq. and Travis Livermore, Esq., of Pappas Grubbs Price PC.
CSHO William Kitchen, of the Mobile, Alabama Area Office.
3000 Old Chemstrand Road, Cantonment, FL 32533.
November 12, 2022.
November 15 and November 17, 2022.
November 15, 2022.
January 6, 2025, in Mobile, Alabama.
April 15, 2025, Atlanta, GA.
(1) the accident (November 12, 2022); (2) OSHA opening its investigation (November 15, 2022); (3) the hearing (January 6, 2025); (4) the decision (April 15, 2025).
On September 20, 2023; the original citation had alleged a violation of 29 C.F.R. § 1926.451(f)(13).
Item 1a, 29 C.F.R. § 1926.451(f)(7) — vacated; Item 1b, 29 C.F.R. § 1926.451(h)(2)(i) — affirmed (as a serious violation, $7000 penalty).

The two violations were grouped for penalty purposes; the proposed total penalty is stated inconsistently in the decision — as $8702 in the introductory "DECISION AND ORDER" section and as $8706 in the "Penalty" section.
A $7000 penalty for Item 1b, Citation 1, which was affirmed as a serious violation (Item 1a was vacated).
Between 25 and 100 individuals (Stipulated Fact 19).
Employees of Coastal Industrial Services, Inc. (Stipulated Fact 18).
A minimum of 10 feet (Stipulated Fact 16).

(1) the cited standard applies; (2) the employer failed to comply with its terms; (3) employees had access to the violative condition; and (4) the employer knew or could have known with reasonable diligence of the condition. JPC Grp., Inc., No. 05-1907, 2009 WL 2567337, at *2 (OSHRC Aug. 11, 2009).

Because the Secretary failed to present persuasive, admissible evidence that PRS employees altered the scaffold; the key third-party (Koch/Coastal) statements were excluded as inadmissible hearsay that did not qualify under the Rule 807 residual exception, and the remaining admitted evidence did not establish noncompliance.
PRS conceded it did not have a competent person on site to oversee construction or alteration of the scaffolds.
PRS did not address it in its post-hearing brief, so the court deemed it waived/abandoned (footnote 2).
Inconsistently: Stipulated Fact 17 names him "Dominga Rocha," while the Findings of Fact refer to "Domingo Rocha," a PRS foreman.
